rulings.Case Title: BASILIO UDARBE ET AL VS. PREMA P. THEKKEK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Show Cause Re Defendants' Contempt Of Court And Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for June 18, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFFS BASILIO UDARBE, JARMILA ROMANOVA, BRANDON THIBEAULT'S Motion For Order To Show Cause Re Defendants' Contempt Of Court And Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiffs Basilio Udarbe et al. seek an order to show cause re defendants' contempt arising out of the court's May 7, 2025, order enforcing the parties' settlement agreement. The court has no proof before it that defendants were ever served with the May 7, 2025 order and therefore does not issue an OSC re contempt, which requires a finding that the defendants had notice of the order and willfully did not comply. Rather, the court sets a hearing re: settlement compliance for July 21, 2026, at 9 a.m, in Department 301 of this court. The court orders all defendants to appear at the hearing personally or remotely over Zoom. Plaintiffs are ordered to serve notice of entry of this order and of the May 7, 2025 order, and to file a proof of service with the court no later than July 14, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: TRICIA GILLESPIE VS. GAETANI REAL ESTATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Defendant Josh Vogel And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Their Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for June 18, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF TRICIA GILLESPIE'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Defendant Josh Vogel And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Their Counsel. Plaintiff Tricia Gillespie's motion to compel defendant Josh Vogel to provide further responses to her first set of form interrogatories, first set of special interrogatories, and first set of requests for production of documents; for a deemed-admitted order as to her first set of requests for admissions; and for sanctions is granted in part. As of the date of Gillespie's reply, no responses had been made. (Swanson Reply Decl., para. 8.) Defendant's opposition states that responses will be made before the hearing. Defendant is ordered to provide objection free, verified responses to the form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production by July 2, 2026. Because the court has no evidence that responses to the requests for admission have been provided, all matters in the first set of requests for admission are deemed admitted by Vogel. Sanctions are warranted; a failure to respond timely to discovery lacks substantial justification. The court awards $2000 in reasonable sanctions. Vogel shall pay the sanction within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BIANCA ESTRADA ET AL VS. CHRISTINA QUEZADA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for June 18, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANTS JAMES SYPULT, TAMARA SYPULT'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is overruled. A demurrer tests whether the challenged cause of action necessarily fails to state a claim for relief. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court liberally construes the complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 452. Defendants contend that the complaint names the wrong location for the alleged accident and that the Doe pleading naming Tamara Sypult as a Doe was improper. On demurrer, the court accepts the facts alleged in the complaint as a true. Defendants point to no judicially noticeable facts that undermine the complaint; rather, they rely on their statements in correspondence with plaintiff. The court cannot consider these on demurrer. The proper vehicle to use extrinsic evidence to test the validity of Doe pleading under Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 474 is a motion to quash service of the summons. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK VS. REBECCA MOLLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Aside Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ryan W. Streckfus; [Proposed Order];
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for June 18, 2026, line 8. DEFENDANTS REBECCA MOLLER, R. MOLLER AND ASSOCIATES, INC'S Motion To Aside Judgment. The motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The court accepts the attorney declaration of fault. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 473, subd. (b).) Although defendants Rebecca Moller et al.'s counsel sent a notice of disengagement, counsel's failure to secure the terms of the settlement agreement for his client by failing to file an answer and make the first appearance fee are attorney mistakes that accrued at the time of the stipulation and before the disengagement. The court orders defendant's counsel to pay $500 to plaintiff as a penalty. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 473, subd. (c)(1).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
